codeReview.summary: The fix removes the immediate multi-row write path by switching active-state writes to DELETE+INSERT, but it does not deterministically recover legacy multi-row states on read/hydration; relaunch can still read an arbitrary row via unordered LIMIT 1 before any write path normalizes the table.

issues.description: fetchActiveProfileState still uses `SELECT ... FROM switcher_active_profile LIMIT 1` with no ordering or canonical-row constraint. Existing databases created by prior behavior can already contain multiple rows (original bug), so relaunch hydration can still resolve a stale/arbitrary activeProfileID until setActiveProfile/selectFallbackActiveProfile is called.

The fix feature claims regression coverage for repeated set/unset and relaunch hydration, but no new test asserts canonical single-row recovery from pre-existing multi-row state; current active-state tests only cover single-step set/clear/fallback paths.

summary: Fix review failed. While commit 6ae375d addresses new multi-row writes, it does not fully close the original nondeterminism for already-corrupted active-profile tables because fetchActiveProfileState still performs unordered LIMIT 1 reads during relaunch hydration.
